messages.content:
You are an expert in writting job application materials for Alfred P. Sloan Foundation, a research focus institution.
    I want you to add a position fit paragraph to the following COVER LETTER START and integrate my fit the institution and department with the INSTITUTION VALUES and ECON DEPARTMENT CONTEXT.
    Keep the length of the paragraph to about 80 words.
    Use a professional and academic tone, but make it engaging and easy to read.
    For each statement of fit add an EXAMPLE from my research, teaching, or service experience that demostrates the fit statement.
    For example, for interdisciplinary research you can add that I won Dedman College Interdisciplinary Institute’s Inaugural Graduate Student Summer Research and Writing Fellowship.
    Avoid using hyphens, bullet points, or numbered lists.
    Please output the full new paragraph as `body_text` and a short chain-of-thought explanation of how you modified the BASE COVER LETTER START to fit the institution and department as `cot`.
    The text should follow the Typst formatting in the BASE COVER LETTER START.

    BASE COVER LETTER START:
    My research explores how entertainment media both reflects and shapes economic behavior. I develop novel methods using generative AI to extract cultural values from television shows and to test how these values affect individuals' behaviors and attitudes.
 
 My job market paper "Does Watching Women Work, Work?" provides causal evidence that teenage exposure to telenovelas featuring empowered female characters increases women's subsequent labor force participation. The effects are driven exclusively by portrayals of women in non-traditional careers and narratives that frame empowerment with joy or anger. A second paper shows that telenovelas focused on inequality reduce support for redistribution by downplaying or trivializing issues of economic disparity. My future work will generalize this approach to measure broader cultural values and use AI to analyze diverse media for a comprehensive study of culture.
 
 CONDITIONAL EXAMPLES:
 [If interdisciplinary research is emphasized] â†’ I was awarded Dedman College Interdisciplinary Institute Graduate Fellowship at Southern Methodist University.
 
 [If service/leadership is valued] â†’ I received the Service Melody Rice Memorial Award and served as President of the Economics Graduate Club at Southern Methodist University.
 
 [If teaching/mentoring is important] â†’ I have extensive experience mentoring, teaching, and tutoring a diverse range of students, including U.S. student-athletes and Chilean undergraduates and graduates.
 
 [If technological fronteir are relevant] â†’ My research demonstrates how AI tools can advance social science research methodologies.
 
 [If reserach fit is emphasized] â†’ My research program on the cultural drivers of economic behavior will broaden INSTITUTION established strengths in FIELD1 and FIELD2, providing a new dimension to the department's work.
 
 [if teaching macro cousers] â†’ extensive experience beign a teaching assitant on varios macro courses in undergrad and graduate school.
 
 [if teaching micro cousers] â†’ experience on beign a instructor in intermediate micro, TA for several game theory classes and econometrics.
 
 [if teaching at all] â†’ experience as an instructor, teachng assitant and mentor to both undergraduate and graduate students in both micro and macro courses.

    JOB ADVERTISEMENT:
    - Recent Ph.D. in economics or closely related field required
- Strong experience with data, coding, and econometrics
- Excellent communication, organizational, and writing skills
- Scholarly judgment, curiosity, and initiative
- Strong econometric, statistical, measurement, and data science skills
- Familiarity with R, Jupyter Notebooks, LaTeX, and open-source resources desirable
- Integrity, professionalism, and discretion
- Ability to thrive with both directed and self-directed tasks
- Interest in developing broad perspectives on economics
- Collaborative and administrative skills; attention to accuracy and detail

    INSTITUTION VALUES:
    

    ECON DEPARTMENT CONTEXT:

    JOB ADVERTISEMENT:
    - Full-time postdoctoral research associate in applied economics (health, environment, education) at University of Illinois Urbanaâ€“Champaign, starting Fall 2026
- Work closely with Dr. David Molitor and Health Care Research Initiative (HCRI)
- Required: Ph.D. in economics or related field by start date; advanced econometrics training; strong programming skills (Stata, R, Python, etc.)
- Preferred: Experience with Medicare/large administrative data, randomized controlled trials, GIS/geospatial analysis, interdisciplinary teamwork, strong writing/communication
- Responsibilities: Collaborate on research, data analysis, supervise RAs, manage data agreements, contribute to HCRI goals, participate in department activities
- Office-based in Champaign, IL; standard full-time schedule; salary $65,000â€“$75,000
- Application deadline: January 6, 2026; apply via https://jobs.illinois.edu

    INSTITUTION VALUES:
    

    ECON DEPARTMENT CONTEXT:

    JOB ADVERTISEMENT:
    - Senior Lecturer or Reader in Economics position at University of St Andrews Business School
- Seeking candidates with an international research reputation in Economics
- Ability to contribute to world-leading, interdisciplinary research and publish in top outlets
- Experience attracting research funding and promoting knowledge exchange
- Excellent teaching skills for diverse undergraduate and postgraduate students
- Engagement with academic, public, private, and third-sector organisations for impactful research and student experience
- Interest in contributing to the new Master in Public Policy programme
- Specialization in applied microeconomics, especially behavioural and experimental economics, preferred
- PhD in Economics or a related discipline required

    JOB ADVERTISEMENT:
    - Postdoctoral position in Economics at Copenhagen Business School (CBS), focusing on gender differences in labor market dynamics and career progression.
- Collaboration with Associate Professor AnnaÃ¯g Morin on empirical and theoretical research using Danish register data.
- Strong background required in labor economics, applied microeconometrics, or macro-labor economics.
- Experience with structural modeling of job search and labor market dynamics is highly valued.
- Proficiency in Stata is required; knowledge of R, Matlab, Python, and/or Fortran is a plus.
- Experience working with micro data, especially administrative or matched employerâ€“employee data, is important.
- Documented research track record at the international level, including publications in recognized journals.
- Professional proficiency in English (written and spoken) is required.
- The position can be research-only (2 years) or include teaching (3 years).
- Candidates should demonstrate enthusiasm for working in a dynamic, international, and business-oriented academic environment.

    JOB ADVERTISEMENT:
    - Required/desired candidate attributes (priority):
    - Ph.D. in Economics or related field with PhD-level economics coursework
    - Interest in development economics, education, or public policy
    - Strong teaching orientation: passion for teaching and experience developing teaching materials
    - Excellent coordination and administrative skills (program design, approvals, logistics)
    - Commitment to policy-relevant, impactful research; ability to balance program work and independent research
    - Self-motivated, dynamic, creative about capacity building and program implementation
    - Professional working proficiency in English (German is a plus)

- Key responsibilities (concise):
    - Lead design and launch of UZH-based graduate programs in partnership with Lemann Center, ADEPT, and faculty (80% of time)
    - Co-design curriculum, manage logistics, obtain approvals, handle admissions and advertising
    - Teach and help organize courses (short professional courses to semester-length)
    - Support ADEPT/Lemann Center partnerships, develop teaching materials, contribute to related research

- Terms and logistics:
    - One-year postdoc position (possible renewal), start by September 2026
    - Based at University of Zurich; applications reviewed rolling from Dec 10, 2025
    - Submit cover letter, CV, job market paper, and â‰¥3 recommendation letters; interviews begin mid-December (online)
